_source: Messaging System v1.3 (Karishni, Wiom) + Taxonomy v1.3 audience-layer review
_note: Three layers, three jobs. Every creative targets exactly one layer. Locked.

L1.name: Ready buyer
L1.description: Has decided to buy home internet and is choosing a provider this week. Active, comparison-mode, incumbent-trained (Jio/Airtel/broadband schema).
L1.objective: Bridge from WiFi-comparison to ghar ka net. Land that Wiom is a different category, not just a different provider.
L1.starting_frame: WiFi, plan, monthly, speed, broadband. Provider-comparison mode.
L1.differentiator: FLEXIBILITY — pay only for days you use. Granularity (1/7/14/28 din) is the proof.
L1.bridge: 'Which WiFi brand do I pick' → 'this is a different way to have home internet' → 'flexibility is why it's worth choosing.'
must_not: Lead with WiFi as standalone noun without RWGKN destination
Compare to Jio / Airtel / ACT by name
Add occasion examples before category is anchored (Static 02 = 21% confusion)
Cram category and flexibility into one beat — one job per creative
proof_structures: Plain category statement first (Static 01 = 60% Net id, 0% confusion)
Show granularity directly — 1 din, 7 din, 14 din, 28 din
Whole-home / family scene over device shots
Resist additions — every feature or comparison costs category clarity
L1.contamination_risk: HIGHEST — burst-then-churn signal. Scale only on recharge quality; cohort-fail overrides comprehension-pass.

L2.name: Blocked buyer
L2.one_liner: I want home internet but its current shape doesn't fit my life.
L2.description: Would buy if the commitment shape were different. Natively fit for Wiom — the recharge model IS their answer.
L2.objective: Land that home internet can be recharge-shaped, and Wiom IS that.
L2.starting_frame: Monthly bill rejected, 'kab kab chalega', variable income, 'lock-in'. Not comparing providers — rejecting the category shape.
L2.bridge: 'Home internet is too rigid for my life' → 'home internet can be recharge-shaped' → 'Wiom IS that.'
must_not: Sound like 'cheaper broadband' — L2 users sense the trap and bounce
Hide the recharge model — the recharge-shape IS the value prop
Add occasion examples before the structure lands
Position as a downgrade ('if you can't afford broadband')
Bury the granularity (1/7/14/28 options are what unblocks them)
proof_structures: Lead with commitment-shape contrast: 'Monthly bill nahi, recharge'
Show granularity as choices (not a price table)
Household / income context as agency, not constraint
Family setting that signals 'built for people whose lives look like yours'

L3.name: Workaround pain
L3.one_liner: Mobile data for the house is painful.
L3.description: Feels the pain but hasn't categorised home internet as the solution. Conversion from mobile-data-pain into home-internet consideration.
L3.objective: Convert mobile-data pain into ghar ka net consideration.
L3.starting_frame: 'Data nikal jata hai', hotspot, 'data finished'. Recharge = mobile. Not thinking about home internet at all.
L3.differentiator: WHOLE-FAMILY-ON-ONE-RECHARGE — 3–4 mobile recharges/week = 1 ghar recharge that serves everyone.
L3.bridge: Pain-first (surface the problem) → ghar ka net as the answer that fits. Two steps: 'this pain has a solution' → 'the solution is ghar ka net — recharge-shaped too.'
must_not: Lead with category before pain — pain is the door for L3
Shame the current behaviour — mobile data has been their solution; respect before replacing
Make it aspirational — 'imagine your family with home internet' is out of scope (Bob's territory)
Compete with mobile providers — frame as 'next level,' not 'alternative'

Let 'recharge' float as head noun without ghar anchor — CRITICAL RULE: 'नेट' must be the destination noun. NEVER 'रिचार्ज' alone as head. Applied IN-CONTEXT (a prior line/visual/lockup that grounds घर-का-नेट satisfies the rule). NOT applied per-line mechanically.
proof_structures: Pain-first opening: trigger recognition before delivering the answer
Math demonstration: 4 mobile recharges/week = 1 ghar recharge for everyone
Two-step framing: name the pain, then introduce the answer
L3.contamination_risk: MODERATE — primary misread risk is mobile-data-product. Ghar anchor is mandatory. L3 creatives typically have slower payback; may need assisted conversion.

l3_critical_note.rule: नेट must be the destination noun. Never रिचार्ज alone as head.
l3_critical_note.application: IN-CONTEXT, not per-line. A prior line, visual, or end-slate lockup that establishes the घर-का-नेट destination satisfies the rule. A subsequent line can lead with रिचार्ज if the destination has been grounded. A creative that has NO घर anchor anywhere fails this rule.
l3_critical_note.source: Messaging System v1.3 §6C + Taxonomy v1.3 audience-layer review + S2 Variant override (June 2026)
l3_critical_note.override_history: Original KILL verdict on S2 Variant applied this rule per-line (mechanically) and was overturned — prior PT line + lockup grounded the destination.

cross_layer_rules.universal_load: The word 'recharge' arrives pre-loaded with the mobile-data schema. Every creative across all three layers must anchor 'recharge' to ghar ka net.
cross_layer_rules.one_job_per_creative: Land one thing cleanly. Category OR flexibility OR proof-of-commitment-shape — one per creative.
cross_layer_rules.category_first_value_second: Category must land before value proposition. When category lands, flexibility transmits. Reverse order fails.
cross_layer_rules.occasion_examples: Do NOT add use-case occasions before category is resolved. Occasions add cognitive load, not clarity. Exception: L2 occasions permitted if and only if category anchors first.
cross_layer_rules.zero_confusion_bar: Static 01 and Static 03 both hit 0% confusion. That is the bar — not 'under 20%'.

comprehension_test_pass_bar.source: Messaging System v1.3 §8. Mandatory pre-launch. Non-negotiable after setting in the brief.
